global_notes:
This editor cuts with a confident, music-first instinct — at 39 cuts across 45.5s (~1.17s average hold), the pacing is brisk but purposeful, suggesting they're hitting most beats without over-cutting into flash territory. The most telling signal is the verdict breakdown: only 4 clips kept as-is from the pipeline, but 12 added — meaning the editor was aggressively expanding coverage rather than culling it, layering in moments the pipeline missed, likely to fill musical phrases and reinforce instructional clarity. The 9 trims indicate they also had zero tolerance for holds that overstayed their welcome.

Camera logic reads as front-for-texture, overhead-for-process: the 23 front vs 16 overhead split suggests front was the default for hero moments — the sear, the plate, anything with visual drama at eye-level — while overhead carried the prep and transformation sequences where spatial clarity (knife work, sauce coverage, color change) mattered more than atmosphere.

The 5 editorial groups point to at least a few multi-shot sequences treated as rhythmic units — probably a technique stretch or the transformation/plating section where momentum builds through rapid-fire intercutting rather than single sustained shots. That kind of grouping is a hallmark of an editor who thinks in phrases, not individual cuts.

Overall: this is a hands-on, opinionated edit — the editor doesn't trust the pipeline to tell the story and fills musical space deliberately. Pacing stays consistent rather than dramatically accelerating, which suits instructional content over pure vibe-driven food content.

beats.beat_description: beauty opener (3 shots): Tongs flipping chicken on grill pan, grill marks visible → Same grill shot, chicken being turned → Chicken thighs on grill, some raw some marked

camera.reasoning: Front angle reveals the three-dimensional char depth of the grill marks and captures steam rising off the chicken surface — overhead would flatten both into a graphic pattern with no sense of heat or sizzle.

duration.reasoning: 3.9s matches the exact window of both title card overlays (KTitle Overlay and the recipe title both run 0.0s–3.9s), holding the hero shot long enough for viewers to read "Grilled Chicken Thighs" before the cut releases.

in_point.reasoning: 32.8s lands mid-flip — the tongs are still in contact and the chicken is turning — giving the cut-in a kinetic hook rather than the static resting pose at 33.0s where motion has already resolved.

beats.effects: Animated zoom in (100.0% -> 136.0%); punch-in/crop (63.0% vs 50% baseline); Reframed (offset h:-0.04 v:-0.06)
beats.effects_reasoning: The 100→136% animated zoom replaces the dolly push a static camera never made, drawing the eye progressively tighter onto the grill marks as the title card sits; the 63% punch-in (vs 50% baseline) removes the pan rim and countertop so only seared chicken fills the scroll-stopper frame; the h:-0.04 v:-0.06 reframe corrects the composition drift that the tighter crop introduced, re-centering the chicken breast rather than leaving dead space at bottom-right.

in_point.reasoning: 24.0s cuts in mid-pour rather than at the lift or tip, dropping the viewer directly into motion so the cut itself reads as the start of the action, not a setup beat.

beats.beat_description: BEAUTY: sliced medium v1

camera.reasoning: The front/eye-level angle shows the knife blade's lateral travel through the breast in profile — the depth of each slice, the angle of the cut, and the fanning accumulation of pieces against the board — which an overhead would collapse into an uninformative top-down view of a cutting board.

duration.reasoning: 1.7s is one musical phrase unit — long enough to read "multiple slices, active knife work, progress" but short enough to function as a transitional glimpse before the next clip (B19I6696.mov at 2.9s) carries the full slicing sequence; it sets context without overstaying.

in_point.reasoning: 30.9s lands mid-stroke with several slices already accumulated on the board, so the first frame immediately communicates result (you can see the fanned pieces) rather than requiring the viewer to watch the knife establish its first cut — the action is already legible in frame 1.

beats.beat_description: BEAUTY: sliced texture close-up

camera.reasoning: Front angle shows the cross-section of the sliced chicken — the juicy interior texture and layered slice depth are only readable from eye-level; overhead would flatten the cuts into silhouettes and lose the moisture cues entirely.

duration.reasoning: 2.9s holds long enough to serve as a logo card (Kitchn branding appears mid-hold in the output timeline) while giving the beauty shot room to register as a satisfying resolution — too short and the logo reads as a flash, too long and the static frame stalls the ending.

in_point.reasoning: 54.8s lands just after the camera completes a slow zoom-in settle (the clip was fully blurred at 52.0s from movement, refocused at 53.0s, and the VLM confirms "slightly zoomed in" by 54.0s) — cutting in at 54.8s captures the sharpest, most composed frame after the lens has stopped moving.
